LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE AUG-24-2018 HEARING ON PLAINTIFF DEVLIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON HIS COMPLAINT FOR BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS DENIED. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING THE EXISTENCE AND/OR TERMS OF A SETTLEMENT. (SEE DEVLIN DEC., PAR. 6, EX. A; EUBANK DEC., PARS. 6-8, EX. 4; EUBANK DISPUTE OF DECLARATION OF TAD DEVLIN, 2:13-4:6.). MS. EUBANK IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTIONS OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, EMAIL: TORREANOM@AOL.COM. (302/RBU) (VACATED PER ORDER FILED 8/28/18)

Plaintiff Devlin'S Motion For Summary Judgment On His Complaint For Breach Of The Settlement Agreement,
Off Calendar Sep-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Vincent S. Green, Esq. of Kaufman Dolowich & Voluck LLP, 310.775.6514, appearing for the Plaintiff;
Alice Eubank, Defendant and self-represented litigant, 415.203.6579, appearing in pro per.

PLAINTIFF TAD DEVLIN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter called, ARGUMENT held.

At the conclusion of argument, the matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/RBU)

**After the hearing, the Court issued the following ruling: Plaintiff Devlin's Motion for Summary Judgment on his complant for breach of the settlement agreement is DENIED. There are no triable issues of material fact regarding the existence and/or terms of a settlement (See Devlin Dec., par. 6, Ex. A; Eubank Dec., pars. 6-8, Ex. 4; Eubank Dispute of Declaration of Tad Devlin, 2:13-4:6). Order signed. (see order for full details)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-24-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted the tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF TAD DEVLIN'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT as follows: Plaintiff Devlin's motion for summary judgment on his complaint for breach of the settlement agreement is DENIED. There are triable issues of material fact regarding the existence and/or terms of a settlement. (See Devlin Dec., par. 6, Ex. A; Eubank Dec., pars. 6-8, Ex. 4; Eubank Dispute of Declaration of Tad Devlin, 2:13-4:6.). Ms. Eubank is required to prepare a proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/RBU)
